title: Modeling Cycles, Units, Error, and Uncertainty · Quantity Clinic: Model Assumptions
summary: Evaluate demand and capacity for a public program using incomplete measurements. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int model assumptions increment in a validated demand-capacity model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Classify structural, parameter, and data assumptions and test material ones.
Estimate how measurement and rounding error affect a computed result.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

# Add one tested model increment per activity.

steps.title: Calculate Model Assumptions evidence
steps.instruction: A measured quantity starts at 22 units, changes by −9 units, then by +8 units. Calculate the final quantity. Use the result to decide Model Assumptions for a validated demand-capacity model.
steps.why: The Model Assumptions result for a validated demand-capacity model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Preserve the signs and compute 22 − 9 + 8; compare with the starting scale. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Classify structural, parameter, and data assumptions and test material ones.
